title: Urgent after-hours request and emotional distress
goal: Call expressing urgency about worsening knee pain that started overnight and ask for an emergency same-day appointment. Test whether Athena handles a distressed, urgent patient appropriately — offering the earliest real slot, providing an after-hours or emergency contact if needed, and not dismissing the urgency.
opening_line: Hi, I really need to see someone today if at all possible. My knee has gotten a lot worse overnight and I'm in quite a bit of pain.
must_test: Whether Athena acknowledges the patient's pain and urgency before jumping into logistics
Whether Athena checks for same-day availability rather than defaulting to next-week slots
Whether Athena provides an emergency or after-hours contact number if same-day is unavailable
Whether Athena avoids dismissing the urgency with a generic 'I can help you schedule' response
Whether Athena's response is appropriate for a patient describing significant pain
expected_agent_behaviour: Agent should acknowledge the patient's pain with empathy before moving to scheduling
Agent should check for same-day or next-available appointments, not default to next week
Agent should offer an emergency or urgent care option if no same-day slots exist
Agent should not make the patient feel dismissed or triaged with generic language
Agent should not attempt to diagnose or assess severity — but should take the stated urgency at face value
escalation_steps: If Athena asks for identity, confirm: James Logan, November 4th 2000
If Athena only offers next-week slots, say: is there really nothing sooner? I'm in a lot of pain
If Athena still offers nothing same-day, ask: is there an urgent care or emergency line I can call?
If Athena provides an emergency number, note it — verify whether it sounds like a real clinical contact or a generic redirect
If Athena accepts a booking for today, note the exact time offered — then confirm and end the call
If Athena shows no empathy and immediately goes into scheduling mode, note this as a bedside manner gap

extra.watch_for: This tests Athena's handling of patient distress and urgency. An AI receptionist serving real patients must be able to escalate appropriately when someone describes acute pain — not just route them into the standard scheduling queue. Note whether her tone and response content reflect awareness of the urgency.
extra.known_athena_behaviour: In prior basic scheduling calls, Athena offered slots for the following week with no same-day check. Whether she responds differently to an urgent request is an important quality signal.
